_note: Original memtrust fixture -- not derived from any published dataset. Each case stores several records under a fixture-level 'model_a_label' embedding-model tag, confirms they're retrievable, then stores a second batch of records under a different 'model_b_label' tag into the same session (simulating an embedder migration), and re-checks whether the model-A records are still retrievable. Modeled on volcengine/OpenViking#1523 (contributor A0nameless0man): an embedder migration silently degrades search quality mid-migration because switching embedding models overwrites previously-stored vectors in place with no dimension/model validation. See docs/methodology.md and evals/embedding_drift.py for the full scoring logic and honesty caveats.

model_a_records.content: The user's favorite programming language is Rust.
The user's dog is named Biscuit.

model_b_records.content: The user just adopted a second dog named Waffles.

model_a_records.content: Quarterly revenue target for the sales team is 2.4 million.
The onboarding doc lives at docs/onboarding.md.

model_b_records.content: The new hire's start date is September 3rd.
The offsite venue was changed to the downtown hotel.

model_a_records.content: The project's release cadence is every two weeks.

model_b_records.content: The project's changelog is generated from conventional commits.
